choices.text: Agent node ↔ Tool node loop via conditional edge. Agent (LLM) decides to call tool or finish; conditional edge routes accordingly; tool runs; result back to agent; agent decides again. Loops until agent returns final answer. Classic pattern: think → act → observe → think. Implemented: add_node('agent', llm_call) + add_node('tool', exec_tool) + add_conditional_edges('agent', should_continue) + add_edge('tool', 'agent'). Standard AI agent architecture.

questions.explanation: Agent ↔ Tool loop via conditional edge. Standard agent architecture.

questions.prompt: Need to dispatch variable number of parallel subtasks. How?

choices.text: Send for dynamic parallel. `from langgraph.constants import Send; def dispatch(state): return [Send('worker', {'task': t}) for t in state['tasks']]; graph.add_conditional_edges('planner', dispatch, ['worker'])`. Returns list of Send objects → each spawns worker node with specific input. All run in parallel; results merge into state. For: variable parallel work (process N items; N varies per query). Standard for fan-out workflows.

questions.explanation: Send objects for dynamic parallel. Returns list from conditional edge; each spawns worker.
